content.full_text:
October 13, 2005

Heather K. Whitham
City of Torrance
Office of the City Attorney
3031 Torrance Blvd.
Torrance, CA 90503-5059

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-163

Dear Ms. Witham:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a) - copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking advice that requires us to evaluate conduct that has already occurred. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you submitted, we decline to provide written advice. Your current question involves, in part, decisions by Airport Commissioners of the City of Torrance which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request.

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Andreas C. Rockas
Counsel, Legal Division

ACR:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\05-163

Enclosure
